creator: mandree@FreeBSD.org
text:
Created attachment 249843
add -ffp-contract=off to CFLAGS to avoid contracting floating point insns to FMA (fused multiply-add) which cause two self-test failures

In order to reproduce, you need to allow FMA instructions. I have been compiling with CPUTYPE?=znver1 in make.conf (or $LOCALBASE/etc/poudriere.d/make.conf, for poudriere), which seems to break the two self-tests. This is with the FreeBSD 14.0-RELEASE amd64 base compiler, clang 16.0.6, and also happens with GCC 13.2.0 from ports.

Removing CPUTYPE from make.conf fixed the self-test failures.

So... digging deeper, I find that adding either of these compiler options in CFLAGS fixes the self-test-failures even though CPUTYPE=znver1 (either one alone is sufficient, no need to use both):

-ffp-contract=off # apparently also helps and might be cheaper.

OR

-mno-fma # no fused multiply add instructions at all


Note: FMA are enabled with -match=haswell or higher for Intel, bdver1 or higher for AMD, or x86-64-v3 or higher for psABI versions (for the latter, see <https://gitlab.com/x86-psABIs/x86-64-ABI>)

My proposal would be to CFLAGS+=-ffp-contract=off - but I don't know if this is bad for ARM64.  What do you think, Dirk?
